title: Maundy Thursday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## April 7, 2023 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

April 7, 2023 was Maundy Thursday, the day before Good Friday in the Christian calendar, falling within Holy Week at the end of the Lenten season. The RCL/BCP readings for Maundy Thursday include Exodus 12:1-14 (the Passover instructions), 1 Corinthians 11:23-26 (Paul's account of the Last Supper institution), and John 13:1-17, 31b-35 (Jesus washing the disciples' feet and the new commandment to love one another). This is one of the most solemn and ritually significant days in the Episcopal liturgical year, traditionally marked by the Maundy Thursday liturgy featuring the ritual foot-washing and stripping of the altar.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church in early April 2023 was in the midst of ongoing debates about sexuality, gender identity, and canonical revision that had intensified over the previous years. The Diocese of Colorado had no major episcopal vacancies or elections at this time, though national conversations about LGBTQ+ inclusion and doctrinal standards continued to occupy church leadership. No specific General Convention actions were immediately pending in early April, with the next General Convention scheduled for summer 2024.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical spring weather patterns in early April with temperatures in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit and occasional rain. The Colorado legislature was in its final weeks of the 2023 session, with debates ongoing over education funding and other budget priorities. Locally, the Capitol Hill neighborhood where Saint John's Cathedral is located continued its ongoing gentrification discussions and development debates that had characterized the area for several years.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The week before April 7 saw continued coverage of the Idaho college murders trial of Bryan Kohberger, with jury selection underway in what was being framed as one of the nation's most closely watched criminal cases. The Trump indictment news cycle continued as former President Trump faced federal charges related to classified documents, with legal proceedings and political fallout dominating national coverage. The debt ceiling crisis was looming as Congress faced April/May deadlines, with negotiations between the Biden administration and House Republicans intensifying over spending and fiscal policy.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Turkey and Syria were still in the acute phase of earthquake recovery following the devastating February 6, 2023 earthquakes; international aid efforts were ongoing but both nations faced massive reconstruction challenges. The war in Ukraine continued with Russian forces conducting spring offensive operations, and discussions about potential peace negotiations remained speculative despite ongoing diplomatic channels. China's relationship with the US was tense following the shooting down of a Chinese surveillance balloon in February, with geopolitical tensions over Taiwan and trade policy remaining elevated.

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in early April 2023 was characterized by post-pandemic normalization with lingering uncertainty about economic direction, healthcare (including lingering COVID concerns), and political polarization. The 2023 NBA season was in its final regular season stretch with playoff implications being decided, and the NCAA Division I Men's Basketball Tournament (March Madness) had concluded just days before, with UConn winning the championship on April 3. Entertainment discourse was beginning to shift toward summer blockbuster season with discussions of upcoming Marvel and other franchise films; the cultural conversation remained heavily influenced by social media discourse on gender, sexuality, and institutional accountability.
